text: "You may be in luck.  I carry a modest amount of adventurer's equipment behind the counter.  Would you like to make a purchase?"
speaker: shopkeeper

text: "Oh, good! I do try to stock some things adventurers can use.  I actually would rather be an adventurer than a shopkeeper, you see."
speaker: shopkeeper

text: "My items are pretty ordinary, though.  You probably already have most of them if you've done any adventuring at all."
speaker: shopkeeper

text: "Now what was that you wanted again?"
speaker: shopkeeper

text: Well, look around anyway.  You may find something you could use."
speaker: shopkeeper

text: You see broom and rake handles in this barrel.
speaker: narrator

text: Flasks, very useful for storing liquids.
speaker: narrator

text: An unornamented but exceptionally sturdy shield.
speaker: narrator

text: The proprietor's teapot.
speaker: narrator

text: Pickled Saurus eggs.
speaker: narrator

text: Linen coarsely woven from the fibers of spore spitting Spirea plants.
speaker: narrator

text: Bags of flour.
speaker: narrator

text: Pickled pigs feet.
speaker: narrator

text: Jars of beets and sauerkraut.
speaker: narrator

text: Tea imported all the way from Shapeir.
speaker: narrator

text: Salt from the desert east of the mountains.
speaker: narrator

text: Sugar from dried figs.
speaker: narrator

text: Salted, dried Saurus.
speaker: narrator

text: Locally grown pepper.
speaker: narrator

text: Wild honey from the forest.
speaker: narrator

text: You add ten gold and sixty silver coins to your money pouch.
speaker: narrator

text: The Kobold seems more interested in frying you with Ball Lightning than in talking to you.
speaker: narrator

text: You'd better not.  This cave is not recommended by the Michelin Guide.
speaker: narrator

text: You don't have time for that now.
speaker: narrator

text: You can't reach the key from here.
speaker: narrator

text: You take the Kobold's glowing key.
speaker: narrator

text: The Kobold won't let you near his key.
speaker: narrator

text: VERY carefully, you remove the key from around the Kobold's neck.
speaker: narrator

text: Uh oh.  You weren't stealthy enough.  You have awoken the Kobold.
speaker: narrator

text: You already have the key.
speaker: narrator

text: You retrieve the dropped key.
speaker: narrator

text: The key is gone.
speaker: narrator

text: The chest is far too heavy to move.
speaker: narrator

text: You've already taken the treasure.
speaker: narrator

text: You are too far away to get the Kobold's treasure.
speaker: narrator

text: You will have to open it first.  The chest seems to be locked.
speaker: narrator

text: The key doesn't fit in the chest.  It must be for a different lock.
speaker: narrator

text: You don't see any locks here.
speaker: narrator

text: You have no skill at picking locks.
speaker: narrator

text: You don't see a chest here.
speaker: narrator

text: No time for that now!
speaker: narrator

text: You pick up the Kobold's mushrooms and put them in your pack.
speaker: narrator

text: The fungus is slimy and firmly attached to the cave walls.  You'd better leave it there.
speaker: narrator

text: Looking back the way you came, you see a faint reflection of the distant sunlight from the cave entrance.
speaker: narrator

text: The stalactites look much like the stalagmites, except that they go the other way.
speaker: narrator

text: This part of the cavern is spacious and multi-leveled.  Phosphorescent fungus illuminates the cavern with an unearthly glow.
speaker: narrator

text: On a rocky platform above the cave floor is a Kobold, one of the race of ugly, miserly creatures known for their skill in Magic.
speaker: narrator

text: The Kobold's large, brass key glows slightly in the dark cavern.
speaker: narrator

text: Some sort of food (mushrooms?) sits in a pile on the stone table.  It looks almost edible... to a Kobold.
speaker: narrator

text: The Kobold's dinner table is now bare.
speaker: narrator

text: The strange creature is dressed in ragged clothes.  It has large, pale eyes.  It must have spent its entire life in these caves!
speaker: narrator

text: A large, brass key hangs from a thong around the creature's neck.
speaker: narrator

text: The Kobold has disappeared entirely.  You are not sure whether you killed him, or whether he escaped by some sort of spell.
speaker: narrator

text: Where the chest was, you see numerous pieces of gold and silver.
speaker: narrator

text: There is no sign of any other treasure in the cavern.
speaker: narrator

text: You don't *see* any treasure here.
speaker: narrator

text: The chest is empty.
speaker: narrator

text: The chest contains numerous pieces of gold and silver.
speaker: narrator

text: You will have to open the chest first.  It seems to be locked.
speaker: narrator

text: Nothing useful remains of the chest.
speaker: narrator

text: You cannot see the chest, but it feels fairly large, and is too heavy to carry.
speaker: narrator

text: You don't *see* a treasure chest here.
speaker: narrator

text: The damp floor is too slippery for you to run safely.
speaker: narrator

text: Just try to walk out of the cave while you still have time.
speaker: narrator

text: That's what you're doing.  Keep going!
speaker: narrator

text: There are no monsters here to attack.
speaker: narrator

text: How embarrassing.  You don't have a weapon with which to fight!
speaker: narrator

text: You're too close to the Kobold for throwing to be practical.
speaker: narrator

text: There's nothing to open here.
speaker: narrator

text: There are no passive spells to trigger here.
speaker: narrator

text: You don't need to cast Fetch -- everything here is in easy reach.
speaker: narrator

text: You just bumped into something on the floor.  It feels like a large wooden chest, but you don't see anything there.
speaker: narrator

text: Wow!  The chest must have been booby-trapped.  You can really feel the damage from the blast.
speaker: narrator

text: "You go!  You no stay here!"
speaker: kobold

text: Your foot slips on the wet cavern floor and makes a scuffing noise.
speaker: narrator

text: The Kobold casts a spell with which you are not familiar.
speaker: narrator

text: Next time you swing at innocent inanimate objects, make sure you are strong enough to survive when they swing back at you!
speaker: narrator

text: You could not withstand the force of the blast from the Kobold's magically protected chest.
speaker: narrator

text: You are overcome by the power of the Kobold Wizard's magic.  You need to either practice your skills more or plan a better attack strategy.
speaker: narrator

text: This section of cave has an eerie quality.
speaker: narrator

text: This large rock covers a Meep hole.
speaker: narrator

text: A furry blue Meep
speaker: narrator

text: A furry purple Meep
speaker: narrator

text: A furry green Meep
speaker: narrator

text: No way!  Those Meeps are too fast and the rocks too hard for you to want to try that again!
speaker: narrator

text: Try going over to where the Meeps are if you really want to hack them into little furry bits.
speaker: narrator

text: You detect no other magic here.
speaker: narrator

text: The Open spell only works on locks and small closed things.
speaker: narrator

text: You don't have a rock
speaker: narrator

text: You can't get that from where you're standing.
speaker: narrator

text: The Meeps would not appreciate it if you tried to take their fur -- the weather here is still very cold.
speaker: narrator

text: You don't see a Spell Scroll here.
speaker: narrator

text: The baby Meep is just too quick for you.  Besides, they eat too much.
speaker: narrator

text: You don't see any baby Meeps at the moment.
speaker: narrator

text: You look at the apple cores, and quickly decide they are of no use to you.  Besides, they look disgusting.
speaker: narrator

text: You are in the Meeps' Peep.  The colorful, furry Meeps timidly pop out of their holes from time to time.
speaker: narrator

text: The baby Meep sure is cute.
speaker: narrator

text: All of the Meeps are hiding deep within their holes.  Your vicious attack has them terrified.
speaker: narrator

text: You realize that you have never seen more than one green Meep at a time.  Perhaps there is only one.
speaker: narrator

text: There seems to be an abundant supply of blue and purple Meeps.  If only they'd stay still for a moment, perhaps you could count them.
speaker: narrator

text: You see blue Meeps, purple Meeps, and occasionally a green Meep.  They seem to be very shy; whenever you approach one, it hides under its rock.
speaker: narrator

text: Large, heavy-looking rocks cover the many holes.  The Meeps must be stronger than they look to lift these rocks so easily.
speaker: narrator

text: You keep seeing differently-colored Meeps come out of each hole.  They either lead to large underground caverns, or they are all connected under the earth.
speaker: narrator

text: The trees and bushes look much like those you've seen elsewhere in the valley.
speaker: narrator

text: The mountains are very steep here.
speaker: narrator

text: The meadow is covered with a light carpet of grass, broken up by the Meep holes.
speaker: narrator

text: There is a wad of bright green fur on the ground.
speaker: narrator

text: The Meeps have brightly-colored, soft-looking fur.
speaker: narrator

text: It looks like a Spell Scroll.
speaker: narrator

text: The scroll is blank now, but once contained the Detect Magic spell.
speaker: narrator

text: You can't talk to any of the Meeps.  They are all hiding deep within their holes, terrified after your vicious attack.
speaker: narrator

text: "Hey, just ask me about anything you want to know."
speaker: meep

text: You don't see any Meeps to talk to at the moment.
speaker: narrator

text: "We use rocks for doors.  They keep us dry and warm."
speaker: meep

text: "We are happy Meeps, living in our happy holes.  Don't worry.  Be happy!"
speaker: meep

text: "Hey, go ahead, take it. It's yours."
speaker: meep

text: "Hey, like fur is good stuff, keeps us warm.  Mine's the best -- it's like green, you know?"
speaker: meep

text: "Gee, boss, I really don't know much about that at all.  Hey, sorry."
speaker: meep

text: Why, that's me!
speaker: meep

text: Man, those rocks are tough!
speaker: narrator

text: You hear squeaky muttering from beneath the ground.  It seems the Meeps are having quite a discussion about you.
speaker: narrator

text: Hiya, hiya.  Nice to seeya 'gain.
speaker: meep

text: Hiya, hiya.  Pleased to meetcha.
speaker: meep

text: "Hey, there ya go.  Hope it helps ya."
speaker: meep

text: "Hey, sorry I couldn't help ya."
speaker: meep

text: Gosh, I don't seem to have anything like that left.  Really sorry 'bout that.
speaker: meep

text: You pick up the pile of soft green fur and carefully place it in your pack.
speaker: narrator

text: You pick up the Spell Scroll.  As the magical runes fade, you find you now know how to cast the "Detect Magic" spell.
speaker: narrator

text: The magical runes fade from the paper before your eye can focus on them.
speaker: narrator

text: That's one mean Ogress!
speaker: narrator

text: There's no time for idle chatter.
speaker: narrator

text: There is a bat, all folded up within his wings, hanging on the wall.
speaker: narrator

text: Is that a SMILE on that spider's face?
speaker: narrator

text: You HATE houses with cobwebs in the corners, especially when there's an immense spider squatting cheerily in the middle of the cobweb.
speaker: narrator

text: The glass seems to distort the view of the outside world.
speaker: narrator

text: There's a lot of SOMETHING cooking away in the huge black cauldron.
speaker: narrator

text: The smell is awful, the color is sickening, and you don't even want to know what's being prepared for dinner.
speaker: narrator

text: The fire burns hot under the kettle.
speaker: narrator

text: The stone hearth and chimney radiates too much heat.
speaker: narrator

text: It's a plain wooden kitchen cupboard.
speaker: narrator

text: The kitchen knives look old but sharp.
speaker: narrator

text: The pile of straw covered by a blanket must be the occupant's bed.
speaker: narrator

text: You furtively grasp the Magic Mirror.
speaker: narrator

text: You don't seem to be able to move.
speaker: narrator

text: You cannot see the witch anywhere.
speaker: narrator

text: You might as well be talking to yourself.
speaker: narrator

text: Your magic is useless here.
speaker: narrator

text: A quick look around shows that this is a creepy place, despite the pot (cauldron?) simmering briskly on the fire.  Look at the size of that spider!
speaker: narrator

text: The small hut has a beamed ceiling.
speaker: narrator

text: The floor is tidy, with a pile of straw in one corner.
speaker: narrator
